message:
docs(readme): correct MissingIndexColumn arity in IoError row (br-f7sjm / fd90.101)

fd90.101: line 804 IoError row listed 'MissingIndexColumn' alongside
the tuple variants 'Csv(...)', 'Json(...)', etc. without parens,
implying it was a unit variant. But crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:113
declares it as MissingIndexColumn(String). The actual unit variant
in the same row, MissingHeaders, is correctly parens-less. Fix:
append (...) to MissingIndexColumn so the README convention matches
the code (MissingHeaders stays unit-typed; MissingIndexColumn now
shows it carries an inner String).

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -801,7 +801,7 @@ Every crate has its own typed error enum, all implementing `std::error::Error` +
 | `ExprError` | fp-expr | `ParseError(String)`, `UnknownColumn(String)`, `UnknownLocal(String)` |
 | `JoinError` | fp-join | wraps `FrameError` + join-specific failures |
 | `GroupByError` | fp-groupby | wraps `FrameError` + aggregation failures |
-| `IoError` | fp-io | `MissingHeaders`, `MissingIndexColumn`, `Csv(...)`, `Json(...)`, `Parquet(...)`, `Excel(...)`, `Arrow(...)`, `Sql(...)` |
+| `IoError` | fp-io | `MissingHeaders`, `MissingIndexColumn(...)`, `Csv(...)`, `Json(...)`, `Parquet(...)`, `Excel(...)`, `Arrow(...)`, `Sql(...)` |
 
 All error types are re-exported through the `frankenpandas` facade crate, so users can pattern-match without importing internal crates:
